methods.description: Process for obtaining and analyzing surrounding land-use types and diversity around study sites using a government dataset and aerial photographs, performed within specified radii using ArcGIS. This method quantifies land-use composition, diversity (Shannon diversity), and the proportion of natural habitat at both field (100m) and landscape (500m) scales.

steps.description: We obtained the amount and composition of surrounding land-use types for each site, from a government data set for the wet tropics region (DSITI, 2016).
steps.searchable_summary: Download or access government land-use dataset for the wet tropics region
outputs: Land-use dataset (amount and composition of types: natural, grazing (natural), grazing (modified), ground crops, tree crops, residential, commercial, water)

steps.description: We calculated landuse diversity (Shannon diversity) of these within two radii around each site: 100 m (field scale)... using ArcGIS.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate Shannon diversity of land use within 100m radius around each site using GIS software
outputs: Land-use Shannon diversity values for each site (100m radius)

steps.description: We calculated landuse diversity (Shannon diversity) of these within two radii around each site: ... and 500 m (landscape scale), using ArcGIS.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate Shannon diversity of land use within 500m radius around each site using GIS software
outputs: Land-use Shannon diversity values for each site (500m radius)

steps.description: We also calculated the proportion of natural habitat at the landscape scale (500 m radius) from aerial photographs using ArcGIS.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate proportion of natural habitat within 500m radius around each site using GIS software and spatial data derived from aerial photographs
inputs: Spatial data representing natural habitat (derived from aerial photographs or land-use dataset)
outputs: Proportion of natural habitat for each site (500m radius)
